extraction.one_line_summary: IMPEY Daryl won the Stage 4 sprint after BETTIOL Alberto's early leadout faded, first World Tour win since 2019 Tour de France.
tactical_patterns: EF and Movistar paced final climb to drop pure sprinters, favoring climbing fastmen like BETTIOL.
WILLIAMS Stephen briefly distanced on final Cat 2 climb but rejoined over summit, exposing race leader vulnerability.
BETTIOL Alberto led out sprint too early and faded, suggesting leadout timing miscalculation on uphill finish.
KRON Andreas sprinted for time bonuses attempting to take race lead, showing GC ambitions through intermediate gains.

rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: First World Tour win since 2019 Tour, positioning himself at front on climbing finish after prolonged absence suggests full recovery from 2021 crash.
rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: negative: Dropped on final Cat 2 climb before recovering, race lead vulnerable when tempo increases on climbs.
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: neutral: Led sprint too early and faded, fitness strong but tactical execution poor on uphill sprint.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Working for BETTIOL on final climb suggests strong enough form to pace on Cat 2 climbs.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Second place on climbing finish confirms good form for uphill sprints.
rider_signals.KRON Andreas: positive: Sprinting for bonuses to challenge GC shows aggressive racing mentality beyond stage hunting.
